channel.title: Winston Lin
channel.description: Winston Lin writes about developer platforms, serverless, migration, and infrastructure product strategy, with case studies from OCI Functions, Kubernetes, and CI/CD.

item.title: AI Support Products Are Workflow Products
item.description: A lot of AI support tooling gets framed as a model problem when the harder and more durable work is really in workflow design.

item.title: Developer Platforms Mostly Fail On Friction
item.description: Missing features get attention, but most adoption problems start with friction that seems too small to matter.

item.title: Feature Registries Are About Operational Clarity
item.description: A good feature registry is less about cataloging and more about making dependency and health questions answerable under pressure.

item.title: Migration Readiness Is A Product Problem
item.description: Teams often talk about migration as field work or services work, but product choices shape how hard a migration feels long before a customer starts.

item.title: Trustworthy AI Products Need Recovery Paths
item.description: The most important part of an AI product is often what happens after the model stops being confidently helpful.

item.title: What Building Internal Platforms Taught Me
item.description: Internal platforms expose product weaknesses quickly because the users are close enough to tell you exactly where the drag is.

item.title: Why I Still Like Small Python Tools
item.description: Small utilities do not get much attention, but they are still one of the best ways to reduce recurring operational drag.
